stories.story: Nine British citizens, consisting of three men, two women, and four children, were arrested at the Turkey-Syria border on Wednesday. The Turkish military stated they were suspected of trying to cross illegally into Syria. The children's ages range from the youngest born in 2013 to the oldest being 10 or 11. All nine individuals possessed British passports.
key_facts: Arrested on Wednesday at Turkey-Syria border
Children's ages: youngest born 2013, oldest 10 or 11
explicit_unknowns: The reason why the group was allegedly trying to enter Syria

stories.story: Syria has been torn by a roughly four-year war between Syrian government forces, Islamist extremist groups, and other rebels. ISIS is among the combatants and has taken over parts of Syria and Iraq for its claimed Islamic caliphate. ISIS is known to recruit Westerners.
key_facts: Combatants include Syrian government forces, Islamist extremist groups, and other rebels
ISIS took over parts of Syria and Iraq for an Islamic caliphate

stories.story: The British Foreign Office stated on Wednesday that it is aware of the arrests and is seeking information from Turkish authorities. Additionally, UK police have charged a man with terror offenses after a trip to Turkey.
key_facts: British Foreign Office seeking information from Turkish authorities on Wednesday
UK police charged a man with terror offenses following a Turkey trip
explicit_unknowns: Whether the man charged with terror offenses is connected to the nine arrested citizens

stories.story: CNN's Gul Tuysuz reported from Istanbul and Elaine Ly reported from London. Jason Hanna contributed to the report.
key_facts: Jason Hanna contributed to the report
